cmdk.title: Commands
overview.quiet: no activity in the last hour

overview.step2Sub: Configure the webhook on Meta with the app URL and token.
welcome.title: HUB RW Meta Hub
welcome.lead: Connect WhatsApp Business, Messenger and Instagram, monitor webhooks in real time and route events to your operation systems.
login.subtitle: Meta channel connection panel
evidence.title: Evidence (App Review)
evidence.desc: Runs real Graph API calls per permission and captures status + x-fb-trace-id + body, to attach to your Meta app review.
evidence.allowWritesHint: Read-only by default. When checked it fires real actions (e.g. send message, create template) — use with care.
err.NO_FALLBACK_TOKEN: This app has no saved Advanced token. Edit the app and paste one, or pick another token source.
err.MISSING_PARAMS: Missing required IDs (e.g. WABA ID / Phone Number ID). Fill them in or use a connected channel.
err.RECIPIENT_REQUIRED: Provide a send recipient to run the WhatsApp write calls.
side.promo: Centralize OAuth, webhooks and forwarding for Meta channels in one operational panel.
onboarding.title: Get started in 3 steps
onboarding.desc: Set up your first Meta app and connect a channel.
onboarding.step1: Create an app with the App ID and App Secret of your Meta app.
onboarding.step2: Configure the webhook in Meta using the URL and Verify Token shown on the app card.
onboarding.step3: Connect a channel — WhatsApp Business, Messenger or Instagram.
hero.title: Connect Meta channels and watch interactions in real time
hero.text: WhatsApp Business, Messenger and Instagram across multiple apps — OAuth authentication, webhook reception and routing to other systems.
connect.title: Connect a channel
connect.desc: Pick the app and authorize the channel via Meta login. The access token stays on the server only.
connect.noApps: No app registered yet. Go to Apps → New app to begin.
channels.title: Connected channels
channels.desc: Each channel receives your app's webhooks directly in this hub.
channels.removeConfirm: Remove this channel? (the subscription on Meta is not undone)
events.title: Live console
events.desc: Updates automatically as the Meta apps send webhooks.
events.emptySub: Send a message to a connected channel.
apps.title: Meta apps
apps.desc: Each app has its own credentials, webhook URL and forwarding destinations.
apps.removeConfirm: Remove this app? Channels connected through it will no longer be identified.
embed.title: Embed buttons (outside the panel)
embed.hint: Paste the code anywhere, or click Test to open the connection in a popup.
form.name: Name
form.igIdPh: empty = uses the Meta App ID
form.igSecretPh: empty = uses the Meta App Secret
form.fallbackTokenHint: Optional. Used as an automatic fallback when a connection (WhatsApp, Messenger or Instagram) doesn't return the expected asset. Create a System User token in Business Manager with the permissions for the channels you use (Pages: pages_show_list/pages_messaging/pages_manage_metadata; WhatsApp: whatsapp_business_management/whatsapp_business_messaging; Instagram: instagram_basic/instagram_manage_messages; and business_management). Stored on the server only.
form.storeToggle: Save interaction history in the panel
form.storeHint: Uncheck to only forward the webhooks (transactional, end to end) — without keeping history in this hub. Useful for privacy/performance.
form.embedToggle: Allow embeddable connection buttons (outside the panel)
form.embedHint: Enables this app's embed code (shown on the card) to place the connection button on any website.
form.fwdHint: The hub relays each webhook of this app to the URLs below (POST with the original body + X-Hub-App header).
config.title: General
config.aboutDesc: This hub is an open-source project maintained by {brand}.
config.aboutP: Use HUB RW to connect Meta apps, validate webhooks and forward events to CRMs, automations, ERPs or internal queues.
guide.title: How to set up your app on Meta
guide.intro: Follow the step-by-step to create your Meta app and connect it to this hub. Each app's exact URLs are shown on its card, in the Apps tab.
guide.urlNote: In Settings → Basic, paste the domain above into App Domains and register the Website platform with the hub URL. In Facebook Login → Settings, paste the OAuth Redirect URIs above — this fixes the "the domain of this URL isn't included in the app's domains" error. Each app's exact webhook URL is on its card; use the same Verify Token on both sides.
guide.s1.b: Go to developers.facebook.com → My Apps → Create app. Choose the Business type and give it a name. You need a Meta for Developers account and, ideally, a Meta Business.
guide.s2.b: In the app, open Settings → Basic. Copy the App ID and App Secret (click Show). Here in the hub, go to Apps → New app and paste them into App ID (Meta) and App Secret. Still in Basic, add this hub's domain under App Domains.
guide.s3.b: Create a strong random token (e.g. in a terminal openssl rand -hex 24) and paste it into the app's Verify Token (webhook) field here. You will use the exact same token when setting up the webhooks on Meta (step 7).
guide.s4.b: Add the WhatsApp product. Under Embedded Signup → Configuration, create a configuration and copy the Config ID. Paste it into the app's Config ID — Embedded Signup (WhatsApp) field. Add the hub domain to the allowed domains.
guide.s5.b: Add the Facebook Login for Business product and create a Messenger-specific Configuration. Warning — critical point: in that configuration's asset, select Pages — do NOT select WhatsApp. If you mistakenly use a WhatsApp Config ID in this field, the OAuth returns a WhatsApp token and no Pages, and the Messenger connection fails. The configuration's permissions must be pages_show_list, pages_read_engagement, pages_manage_metadata, pages_messaging, business_management. Copy the Config ID of that Pages configuration into the app's Config ID — Login for Business (Messenger) field here. Under Facebook Login → Settings, add the hub domain to Valid OAuth Redirect URIs.
guide.s6.b: Add the Instagram product and enable the API with Instagram Login. In the configuration, in the OAuth Redirect URI field, paste the IG Redirect shown above (and on the app card). Scopes: instagram_business_basic, instagram_business_manage_messages, instagram_business_manage_comments. If the Instagram app has its own ID/Secret, fill the app's Instagram fields; otherwise leave them empty (it uses the Meta App ID/Secret).
guide.s7.b: For each product (WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram), go to Webhooks and set the Callback URL to the app's webhook URL (on the app card, format .../webhook/app/YOUR_APP_ID) and the Verify Token from step 3. Click Verify and save. Then, under Fields (subscribe), subscribe at least messages (and comments for Instagram).
guide.s8.b: Done! Go back to the Channels tab, select the app and click Connect on the desired channel. The hub performs the OAuth, creates the channel and subscribes the webhooks automatically. Interactions show up in the Interactions tab.
guide.callout.b:
The Config ID — Login for Business (Messenger) field requires a Facebook Login for Business whose asset is Pages (not WhatsApp). Each Meta product has its own Config ID: the WhatsApp one (Embedded Signup) goes in the WhatsApp field, and the Pages one goes in the Messenger field. Swapping them makes the OAuth return the wrong asset — a WhatsApp Config ID pasted into Messenger returns a WhatsApp token and no Pages. Permissions required in the Pages configuration: pages_show_list, pages_read_engagement, pages_manage_metadata, pages_messaging, business_management.

Plan B — Business Manager token: if the Pages config uses a System User token and your business already did the WhatsApp Embedded Signup, Meta may return the existing system-user token (WhatsApp-scoped) even with the correct config. In that case, create a System User token in Business Manager (Business Settings → System Users → Generate token) with the Pages assigned and the Pages permissions above, and paste it into Advanced token (Messenger fallback) in the app form. The hub uses that token to resolve Pages when the login returns none. You can also paste the token directly in the Messenger popup ('Connect with a token').

err.APP_NOT_CONFIGURED: This app needs an App ID and App Secret.
err.NO_PAGES: No Page found. Make sure your account manages a Facebook Page and that you granted access to it (pages_show_list) in the popup.
err.MISSING_IDS: WhatsApp did not return the IDs. Finish the Embedded Signup by choosing a number.
err.INVALID_STATE: Invalid session. Restart from the Apps tab.
err.token_exchange_failed: Failed to exchange the code for a token.
cpop.sdkFail: Facebook SDK failed to load. Please try again.
cpop.waiting: Waiting for authorization in the Facebook popup…
cpop.waba.title: Connect WhatsApp Business
cpop.waba.ready: Ready. Click Connect to authorize WhatsApp Business.
cpop.waba.configMissingMsg: WhatsApp Embedded Signup requires a Config ID.
cpop.waba.configMissingInfo:
To connect WhatsApp Business, this app needs an Embedded Signup Config ID.

Go to Apps → edit the app → "Config ID — Embedded Signup (WhatsApp)" field, save and try again.
cpop.waba.configMissingBtn: Embedded Signup Config ID not configured.
cpop.msgr.title: Connect Messenger
cpop.msgr.ready: Ready. Click Connect to choose the Page.
cpop.msgr.noPages: No Facebook Page was found. Make sure you selected a Page when authorizing and that the Messenger configuration requests the 'pages_show_list' permission (and the Page is added to the app as a Business Manager asset).
cpop.msgr.wrongConfigWaba: This configuration returned a WhatsApp token, not Facebook Pages. The Messenger Config ID looks like a WhatsApp Embedded Signup config. Edit the app and use a Facebook Login for Business Config ID (Messenger).
cpop.ig.title: Connect Instagram
cpop.ig.intro: Click Connect to authorize your Instagram Business account via Instagram login.
result.invalidSessionMsg: The state expired or the app no longer exists. Restart from the panel.
result.igAppNotConfigured: Set the Instagram App ID (or the Meta App ID) in this app.
result.codeMissingMsg: Instagram did not return an authorization code.
result.subFail: Channel created, but the subscription failed: {err}
result.unexpectedMsg: Failed to connect Instagram. Please try again.
result.unavailableMsg: Embedded connection is not enabled for this app.
result.invalidChannelMsg: Use a valid channel: waba, messenger or instagram.
result.appNoSecretMsg: This app has no App ID/Secret yet.
